query: What is the current national norovirus category on the WastewaterSCAN dashboard as of today (High/Medium/Low), and is it rising, falling, or stable? Note the unusual summer 2026 GII.17 off-season surge.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Answer: Current National Norovirus Status on WastewaterSCAN (as of July 9, 2026)

**The most authoritative reading — the WastewaterSCAN nationwide newsletter (samples through June 30, 2026) — places national norovirus (GII) at MEDIUM and FALLING** (downward 21-day trend), with median concentration down ~37% versus May. This reflects the tail of the receding 2025–2026 winter wave.

**Key nuance:** There's a tension between the official newsletter category and the media narrative:
- **Official newsletters (May & June 2026):** MEDIUM, declining, ~31% then ~37% month-over-month drops. No mention of GII.17 or a summer surge (they track total Noro GII, not genotypes).
- **Media coverage (iHeart May 29; NBC News; Virus Watcher July):** Describe an unusual **off-season summer surge** rising toward "high," especially in the **Northeast**, with ≥12 states active (led by Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina, Pennsylvania), driven by cruise-ship outbreaks, camps, and shellfish.

**On the GII.17 off-season pattern:** Per CDC EID (July 2025), GII.17 jumped from **<10% of outbreaks (2022-23) to 75% (2024-25)**, surpassing GII.4 as the dominant U.S. genotype and pushing seasons earlier. Because GII.17 partially evades prior population immunity, it's cited as a driver of unusual, earlier, and off-season activity — consistent with the summer 2026 surge narrative.

**Reconciliation:** The summer surge is real at the outbreak/regional level, but as of the June 30 official cutoff it had **not flipped the national newsletter category to "high"** — it remained MEDIUM/declining against the winter baseline. The live dashboard (a JS chart I couldn't text-extract) may show a different short-term early-July reading. I flagged data-vintage caveats where some media pieces appear to recycle 2024-25 season figures.

sources.title: Nationwide WastewaterSCAN June 2026 Update
sources.snippet: National Norovirus GII = MEDIUM; downward 21-day trend; median concentration down ~37% vs May 2026; 99% detection rate.

sources.title: Nationwide WastewaterSCAN May 2026 Update
sources.snippet: National Norovirus = MEDIUM, downward; ~31% below April; 34 Low / 55 Medium / 50 High / 8 insufficient sites.

sources.title: Vomiting virus is spreading across most of the U.S.
sources.snippet: Upward trend to 'high' levels across much of the country, rising in the Northeast; GII.17 ~75% of outbreaks.

sources.title: High Levels Of 'Winter Vomiting Disease' Found In Wastewater Across The US
sources.snippet: Norovirus spreading rapidly, cases sharply rising as summer begins, per WastewaterSCAN dashboard (May 29, 2026).

sources.title: Norovirus Summer 2026: Which States Are Seeing the Highest Activity?
sources.snippet: Unusual summer surge active early July 2026; ≥12 states; leaders Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina, Pennsylvania; cruise/camp/shellfish drivers.

sources.title: Increasing Predominance of Norovirus GII.17 over GII.4, United States, 2022-2025
sources.snippet: GII.17 outbreaks rose from <10% (2022-23) to 75% (2024-25), surpassing GII.4; season started earlier in 2024-25.

sources.title: Increased circulation of GII.17 noroviruses, six European countries and the United States, 2023 to 2024
sources.snippet: GII.17 rose to 17-64% of GII detections across Austria, Germany, France, Ireland, Netherlands, England, and the US in 2023/24.
